tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests combine behavioral simulation (actually executing Python code that demonstrates the bug pattern and fix) with AST structural verification (parsing and walking the AST). They don't just grep for text strings - they execute subprocess Python code and verify AST structure.

test_not_tautological.reason: The behavioral portions of the f2p tests are self-contained simulations that don't actually test the agent's code changes. For example, test_rocm_env_setup_not_gated_on_use_rocm_aiter simulates the bug and fix inline. The structural AST checks do verify actual file changes, but a stub that just removes 'use_rocm_aiter' from the condition (without understanding why) would pass. More critically, the behavioral simulations always pass regardless of what the agent does to the files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction describes three behaviors: (1) env vars set for all ROCm cases, (2) cleanup resets class-level caches, (3) no direct dict mutation. All three are tested by the f2p tests, plus regression tests verify existing functionality is preserved.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass), descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code fixes, not structured data output.
